commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Settings view to sync the native Settings window toolbar appearance with the app appearance on view appear and SwiftUI color-scheme changes.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: current main has Settings window resizing but no appearance sync path, and the PR screenshots show the stale toolbar mismatch. I did not run a live macOS appearance toggle in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No repair lane is needed because the PR has no discrete blocking finding; remaining handling is normal maintainer review and CI completion.

target.title: fix: keep Settings toolbar in sync with dark mode switch
target.body: ## Summary The SwiftUI `Settings` scene's native toolbar (the tab bar with the General/Providers/Display/… tabs) can keep a stale **light** appearance after the system theme switches while the Settings window already exists. The result is a light toolbar over dark content (and vice-versa). The content panes themselves are correct — they only use semantic colors — and the app never force-sets an appearance anywhere, so the mismatch is purely the toolbar not tracking the live `effectiveAppearance`. This is aggravated by the `Settings` scene + keepalive `WindowGroup` combination that exists specifically to render those native toolbar tabs. ## Fix Pin the Settings window to `NSApp.effectiveAppearance`: - on `onAppear` (covers opening the window while already in dark mode), and - on `onChange(of: colorScheme)` (covers flipping the system theme while the window is open). Refactored the existing window lookup in `resizeSettingsWindow` into a shared `settingsWindow()` helper that the new `syncSettingsWindowAppearance()` reuses. No behavior change beyond appearance syncing. ## Commands run - `make check` (SwiftFormat + `swiftlint --strict`) → 0 violations - `swift build` → Build complete ## Notes / testing UI-only AppKit change; not unit-testable through the existing model seams. To verify manually: open Settings, then toggle System Settings ▸ Appearance between Light and Dark — the toolbar now follows the content instead of getting stuck. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: hhh2210
